title: E-001 walks: cold-start happy path reaches p1-bootstrap/p1-spot-1
description: Drove scenario E-001 via the in-process MCP harness against the production paths/01-orderbook-viewer manifest. Sequence: assert state.json absent at start, call start (returns outputStyleOk=true and the registry's PathInfo for 01-orderbook-viewer with no warnings), call selectPath which returns personalizationPrompts of exactly 2 entries (poll_interval_ms with range 1000-30000 default 3000, pool_subset enum [both, DEEP_SUI, SUI_USDC] default both), call setPersonalization with values:{} (the 'Use defaults' equivalent, which the SDK schema explicitly accepts and which writes the merged defaults into state), call nextSpot which returns phase.id='p1-bootstrap' spot.id='p1-spot-1' target_file='src/App.tsx' target_range='39-58' with doc_links resolved as path entries and rungs object pointing at rungs/p1-spot-1/{hint,reference,auto}.md. After nextSpot, state.json exists at <project>/.sui-deepbook-course/state.json with cursor.phase_id='p1-bootstrap' and cursor.spot_id='p1-spot-1'. The substituted prompt for the default pool_subset='both' literally reads 'You are configuring for pool: both', so the {{ pool_subset }} substitution path is exercised even on the defaults branch. Note: the scenario's step 4 ('assert all 8 preflight probes report pass') is not satisfied by the start tool itself — start.ts returns preflight:{skipped:true,reason:'cycle-1'}. The skill is responsible for iterating the 8 probes via separate runPreflightProbe calls (documented in MCP Tools section). That is consistent with the spec's communication pattern but worth knowing for the harness implementer driving the cycle-e2e-pass script: the assertion needs to be made against 8 individual runPreflightProbe responses, not the start envelope. Scenario covered: E-001.
impact: E-001 is walkable end-to-end against the deliverable. Personalization defaulting via empty values is observable; state.json persistence is observable; doc_links surface as structured path entries. The acceptance criteria in covers_contract (AC-1.2 outputStyleOk gate, AC-2.1 preflight pass/fail shape via runPreflightProbe, AC-4.2 doc_link resolution + wait state, AC-6.1/6.2 personalization prompt + Use defaults, AC-7.4 absent state file = first run) are all observable in the deliverable, with the caveat that AC-2.1 lives in runPreflightProbe (not start) — the skill must walk the probes individually.
recommendation: Keep the harness expectation explicit: drive runPreflightProbe(probeId) for each of the 8 PROBE_ORDER ids and aggregate, rather than reading start.preflight (which is a placeholder envelope). No code change required for E-001 to pass given this aggregation step. Optionally note in the skill body that 'preflight pass' = all 8 individual probes returning pass:true in sequence.
evidence: next.phase.id === 'p1-bootstrap' && next.spot.id === 'p1-spot-1' && next.spot.target_range === '39-58' && fs.existsSync(stateFile) === true (scenario E-001)

title: E-005 walks: synthetic 04-fake-path is discovered and selectable without code change
description: Drove scenario E-005 by writing a synthetic paths/04-fake-path/path.json (slug=04-fake-path, valid schema with one personalization option and pnpm build command) and a paths/04-fake-path/phases.json (one phase 'p1' with one stub spot 's1') alongside the real 01-orderbook-viewer. Calling start returned a paths array with both entries, sorted slugs equal to ['01-orderbook-viewer','04-fake-path'], and an empty warnings array — confirming the registry surfaces the new path purely from a directory scan with no plugin code changes (AC-3.1). selectPath('04-fake-path') returned ok:true with personalizationPrompts containing the single declared option (poll_interval_ms with range from path.json), confirming the registry → selectPath integration works for any well-formed slug. The scanRegistry implementation reads paths/<slug>/path.json, validates schema, then reads paths/<slug>/phases.json and validates schema; if either is malformed the path is skipped with a structured warning instead of crashing — independently verified against the existing T-022/T-040/T-041 contract tests, all green. Scenario covered: E-005.
impact: Drop-in path extensibility (Vision item 8) is verified at the e2e seam. AC-3.1 holds: a new paths/<slug>/ directory with valid JSON makes the slug appear in selection and selectable, with no source-code edits. This is the load-bearing parametricity proof for future path content drops (02-fee-rebate-swap, 03-dca-vault).
recommendation: Optionally extend the harness to also walk phases.json validation failures (already covered by T-040/T-041 unit tests). No change needed for E-005 itself.

title: E-014 cannot pass: production phases.json p3 is a stub with target_range 1-1, not the spec's p3-poll/116-145 with poll_interval_ms substitution
description: Drove scenario E-014 against the production manifest. Phase 1 substitution works correctly: with personalization {poll_interval_ms:5000, pool_subset:'DEEP_SUI'}, nextSpot at p1-bootstrap renders the prompt 'You are configuring for pool: DEEP_SUI.' and contains zero residual {{ pool_subset }} placeholder — confirming substitutePromptOnly() touches only the prompt and leaves target_file ('src/App.tsx') and target_range ('39-58') unchanged byte-for-byte (AC-6.3 first half holds at p1). However the scenario's load-bearing assertion is at p3: 'walk to phase p3-poll and request the spot ... assert the rendered prompt for p3-spot-1 contains "5000" (substituted into the polling-interval clause) NOT the literal {{ poll_interval_ms }} ... assert target_file ("src/App.tsx") and target_range ("116-145") are unchanged from the manifest defaults'. The shipped phases.json has phase id 'p3-display' (not 'p3-poll' as the spec/scenario require), with spot p3-spot-1 carrying target_range '1-1' (not '116-145'), prompt 'TBD in cycle 5+: Render the order book data in the UI component.' (no {{ poll_interval_ms }} placeholder), and no rungs or doc_links. After advancing through verifySpot twice (with verify stub pass:true), nextSpot at the third position returned phase.id='p3-display' spot.target_range='1-1'. The scenario therefore cannot pass: the phase id literal mismatches, the target_range literal mismatches, and the prompt contains no placeholder for substitution to demonstrate. This contradicts spec.md lines 287-290 and 410-411 which require p3-spot-1 target_range 116-145 and the {{ poll_interval_ms }} placeholder in the polling-tick prompt.
impact: E-014 is the only scenario that proves substitution flows through to a downstream phase prompt, and AC-6.3's second clause (substitution surfaces in 'subsequent phase prompts as {{ poll_interval_ms }} style substitution') is unreachable at p3 with the current manifest. The cycle-e2e-pass.sh harness will fail this scenario at runtime when it tries to assert phase_id='p3-poll' — which is the correct outcome (the manifest is wrong, not the harness). Cycle 6 must update phases.json to materialize p2-retry/p3-poll with the spec's target ranges and prompt placeholders, or the spec must be amended to mark p2/p3 as future work. The mismatch also blocks E-015 (see R1-004) and the help-ladder scenario E-004's 'cursor advanced to p2-retry/p2-spot-1' assertion.
recommendation: Update paths/01-orderbook-viewer/phases.json so phase ids and spot manifests match the spec's normative section (Registry Schema phases.json example, lines 230-294 of spec.md): phase id 'p2-retry' (not 'p2-polling') with target_range '103-114', phase id 'p3-poll' (not 'p3-display') with target_range '116-145' and a prompt embedding '{{ poll_interval_ms }}' such as 'midPrice + getLevel2TicksFromMid every {{ poll_interval_ms }} ms'. Author the rungs/p2-spot-1/* and rungs/p3-spot-1/* content packs and the phases/p2.md, phases/p3.md explainers. Until that ships, mark E-014 (and E-004's 'p2-retry' clause and E-015's 'p2-retry' cursor seed) as expected-fail in cycle-e2e-pass and surface them as remediation work rather than as a green pass.
evidence: phases.json defines: { "id": "p3-display", ... "target_range": "1-1", "prompt": "TBD in cycle 5+: Render the order book data in the UI component." } — scenario E-014 expects phase_id 'p3-poll', target_range '116-145', and substitution of poll_interval_ms=5000 into the prompt

title: E-015 cannot pass: seeded cursor 'p2-retry'/'p2-spot-1' is unreachable because manifest uses 'p2-polling'
description: Drove scenario E-015 by seeding <project>/.sui-deepbook-course/state.json with the exact preconditions from scenarios.json (and matching tests/fixtures/state/valid-cursor-p2.json verbatim): schema_version=1, selected_path='01-orderbook-viewer', cursor={phase_id:'p2-retry', spot_id:'p2-spot-1'}, ladder.p1-spot-1.hint_used=true. Calling start returned the state intact with cursor.phase_id='p2-retry' preserved exactly (state.ts loadState round-trips correctly — AC-7.1 first clause holds and ladder is preserved exactly). Calling nextSpot, however, returned {done: true} with no phase or spot, because phaseEngine.ts getCurrentSpot scans the loaded phases (which are 'p1-bootstrap', 'p2-polling', 'p3-display') for phase.id==='p2-retry' and finds nothing, then returns done:true. The scenario's load-bearing assertion 'nextSpot returns phase_id "p2-retry" / spot_id "p2-spot-1"' therefore cannot hold. The state is round-tripped correctly (the persistence half of AC-7.1 is fine), but resumption against the live manifest yields done:true instead of the expected resume-at-p2-retry view. This is symptomatic of the same root cause as R1-003: production phases.json uses 'p2-polling'/'p3-display' while spec.md, scenarios.json, and the test fixture all use 'p2-retry'/'p3-poll'.
impact: E-015 is the canonical resume-on-restart proof for AC-7.1. With the current manifest, a returning student whose previous session left cursor at p2-retry would silently skip to done:true, which the conductor would interpret as 'lesson finished' — a real, observable bug for any student in the wild whose state.json predates the manifest rename. The fixture tests/fixtures/state/valid-cursor-p2.json was authored against the spec's phase ids, so the cycle-2 contract test for state I/O passes (because it only checks loadState round-tripping), but the cycle-e2e walk through nextSpot exposes the manifest/state divergence. This is the same defect class as R1-003 surfaced from the resume side instead of the personalization side.
recommendation: Same as R1-003: rename phases in paths/01-orderbook-viewer/phases.json to 'p2-retry' and 'p3-poll' to align with spec.md and scenarios.json. Once the rename ships, E-015 passes as written. Alternatively, if the team chooses to keep 'p2-polling'/'p3-display' as the canonical names, update spec.md (Registry Schema phases.json example, AC text), scenarios.json (E-004, E-014, E-015), and tests/fixtures/state/valid-cursor-p2.json all in lockstep — but the spec already takes a strong normative position on 'p2-retry'/'p3-poll' (lines 230-294, 410-411), so the manifest is the right place to fix.
evidence: Seeded state.cursor={phase_id:'p2-retry', spot_id:'p2-spot-1'}; nextSpot returned {done: true} because production phases.json declares 'p2-polling' and 'p3-display' instead of the spec's 'p2-retry' and 'p3-poll' (scenario E-015)
